citations.narrative:
The registrant accepted two shipments of dogs that either did not have a health certificate or had a health certificate that
was dated more than 10 days before the date of shipment.
*AWB 12661456 shipped on 5-July-2025- two dogs were accepted with a health certificate dated more than 10 days
before shipment
*AWB 58930966 shipped on 11-October-2025- one dog was accepted without a health certificate, only a print out of the
dog's medical records from a veterinary clinic
Failure to have receive shipments of dogs with a health certificate does not ensure that the animals being transported are
healthy enough for the rigors of travel and are free of communicable or contagious diseases.
A carrier must not receive any live dog, cat or nonhuman primate for transportation unless and until it is accompanied by a
health certificate issued within 10 days of shipment by a licensed veterinarian.

The registrant did not maintain the record of a health certificate or consignor information for one dog shipped from CID to
PHX on 18-December-2025 (no airway bill available). Health certificates are essential for ensuring the health status of the
animal being transported and consignor information for disease traceability can be reviewed by APHIS officials. No carrier
shall for a period of 1 year, destroy or dispose of any records required to be kept and maintained.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
Additional Inspectors:
Jennifer Walker, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.78(c) 
   
Health certification and identification. 
The registrant accepted two shipments of dogs that either did not have a health certificate or had a health certificate that 
was dated more than 10 days before the date of shipment.  
*AWB 12661456 shipped on 5-July-2025- two dogs were accepted with a health certificate dated more than 10 days 
before shipment 
*AWB 58930966 shipped on 11-October-2025- one dog was accepted without a health certificate, only a print out of the 
dog's medical records from a veterinary clinic 
Failure to have receive shipments of dogs with a health certificate does not ensure that the animals being transported are 
healthy enough for the rigors of travel and are free of communicable or contagious diseases.  
A carrier must not receive any live dog, cat or nonhuman primate for transportation unless and until it is accompanied by a 
health certificate issued within 10 days of shipment by a licensed veterinarian. 
2.80(a) 
   
Records, disposition. 
The registrant did not maintain the record of a health certificate or consignor information for one dog shipped from CID to 
PHX on 18-December-2025 (no airway bill available). Health certificates are essential for ensuring the health status of the 
animal being transported and consignor information for disease traceability can be reviewed by APHIS officials. No carrier 
shall for a period of 1 year, destroy or dispose of any records required to be kept and maintained. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
Jennifer Walker, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
